posts.author: Kathleenharris
posts.content: Eye Progression: Not so great. Since I have been feeling worse the last month or so my eye symptoms have gotten worse again. Mostly when I focus on something it’s like my vision is shaking every so slightly. Having some highlights flash. Still have the distortion in my right eye and of course floaters. Vision just off in general. Could be why dizinesss is worse last week too. Also have blue sclera first noticed in 2020 ish. New: I am experiencing a brand-new staticky, flickering spot in off to the left of my central vision in my left eye now, it’s still very faint but kinda how it started out in my right eye. I know this means my retinal photoreceptors are actively losing their microvascular blood supply and suffocating due to my known issues with microclotting, thick blood, and Bartonella-driven endothelial inflammation etc. tissues are desert. I suspect the blood-retinal barrier is actively leaking, forming a fibrous scar matrix that is creating mechanical traction on my macula. My retina is being choked off again. Thinking back my eye and eyelid also did recieve some vibration about 4 days ago when I was using a zvibe on my ear for vagus nerve, possibly could have sheared the already fragile tissue and caused lesion or tear. This has always been a worry of mine as I don’t want to lose vision. The last time it happened with right eye was scary. I want to know how to deal with it best. I plan to get another Oct asap to compare to my last one over a year ago. In the meantime…

posts.author: Evee
posts.content: Starting a journal: It honestly scares me to list out all the health problems I have. I’ll just say I know I’m a biophysics train wreck and Jack’s cradle cap article is basically my autobiography (yes, I definitely had that very badly as a baby, my mom always remarked that to me, and that article explains everything about my life.) I know I have to leave Southern California. I thought I could get away with taking long trips away from here to offset it (Quintana Roo and El Salvador so far) but I am still a biophysics train wreck and I’m in the middle of a diagnostic nightmare where I have to stay here in the short term to figure out what is going on with me. (Having autoimmune issue -probably dermatomyositis - which can, in some cases, be a sign of paraneoplastic syndrome or cause ILD - though my breathing feels OK - but I need to make sure that it’s “just” autoimmune before I skip town) But I bought a tent and my plan is to spend as much time grounded outside as possible while I am going between tests. I am freelance and taking a break from work short term, so I figured if I only have to be in the city about once a week-ish, in the meantime, I’m going to drive to the most remote healthy locations I can get to and sleep/live outside away from the SoCal nnEMF Last week I went up to Yosemite. On the east side there’s a volcano called Mount Dana, I have no idea what the natural magnetic fields are, but I was just guessing (Plus, it’s easy to away from cell signal, if you’re not i…

posts.author: SaigonTwin
posts.content: Walk around the outside and see where it is coming from. Normally one side is the strongest, then look for towers. If no towers on that side close by good chance it is the neighbors WiFi. In single story buildings shielding the fence with wire mesh (metal window screen) can be a quick easy fix.

posts.author: solamor
posts.content: Here's my journey so far: I grew up in a small farming community in Kansas. H31 haplotype. Almost 100% western European ancestry with the exception of a very small percentage of Coptic Egyptian. And a good portion of Neanderthal. We ate food and animals from our farm. My mother is 95, still lives alone and drives, in spite of living a not very circadian friendly lifestyle. I think her saving grace is that she was a stay-at-home mom who did lots of gardening and avoided nnEMFs. Her only health issue was when she was a child she contracted St. Vitus Dance and missed a year of school. I think the illness coincided with the building of an airport not far from her farm for B-29s during the war. My grandmother had T2 diabetes and congestive heart failure. My health issues started when I got married to an MD and got mixed up in the centralized medicine grinder. When I moved to Los Angeles in the 80's, I developed Graves Disease (my father also had Graves), and the only option I was given was to have RAI. I've been on thyroid meds since. Ten years later, a routine lab led to me being prescribed a statin. I think at the time my LDL was 300, HDL 105, and Tris 75. I developed muscle pain and the cardiologist said I was working out too hard. Then brain fog, and more pain, and my husband thought I might have MS because I was always tripping. Went back for blood tests, had rhabdomyolysis. The statin was changed. Still had pain and horrible brain fog with that prescription. Finally in 2017…

posts.author: Martin Alvarez
posts.content: Ok, I'll go chronologically. My grandmother was always a weak person; she suffered from migraines and died of esophageal cancer. My mother had various spontaneous abortions before having me, but then doctors discovered she had Antiphospholipid Syndrome. They gave her medication, and she was able to give birth to me and my brothers. I was born with retinoblastoma, which was discovered when I was one year old. It was treated with chemotherapy and went into partial remission, but then it started regrowing, so by the age of two, I had my eye removed. After that, my life and health were 'normal.' I think I've always been kind of low on dopamine because I since young i had an addiction to computer games, and on the pandemics I switched that to an addiction to food. In high school, I’ve had a group of gamer friends. During that time, I started to procrastinate and play games when I was supposed to study. Since puberty, I started having acne. When the pandemic started in 2020, I learned about the paleo diet and got rid of my acne, but my over-control and uncompromising perfectionism led to a Binge Eating Disorder. In 2023, I started working in an office right next to a cell tower, and my health started going downhill. I got gut issues (since that year, my gut gets really bloated) when I eat too many carbs, but I can't stop craving them. Also, I got hemorrhoids that hurt when I ate too much, cycles of constipation and diarrhea, and finally, my sleep quality started to decline. Early l…
